articles.title: AP May Face Thunderstorms While Heatwave Over The State Continues - Deccan Chronicle
articles.full_text:
The highest temperature recorded on Sunday is 44.3°C at Atchampet in Palnadu district.
Visakhapatnam: Andhra Pradesh is expected to witness a mix of thunderstorms and intense heat on Monday, according to the Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) managing director Prakhar Jain.
Elaborating, he explained that a trough extending from a cyclonic circulation over Vidarbha across north coastal Andhra Pradesh and the adjoining west-central and northwest Bay of Bengal is influencing the weather over Andhra Pradesh.
APSDMA has advised people to avoid taking shelter under trees, electric poles and hoardings during the thunderstorms. Farmers and shepherds have been asked to remain alert and move to safer locations once they spot dark clouds and witness lightning and thunder.
Temperatures ranging between 42°C and 44°C are likely in parts of Polavaram, Eluru, Krishna, NTR, Guntur, Markapuram, Bapatla, Palnadu, Prakasam, and Nellore districts. Temperatures between 40°C and 42°C are expected in parts of Parvathipuram Manyam, Alluri Sitarama Raju, East Godavari, West Godavari, Nandyal, YSR Kadapa, and Tirupati districts.
Authorities have asked residents to take precautions against the heat. They must also remain vigilant due to the possibility of sudden thunderstorms and lightning.
